---
title: "Sketched and AR-aware models"
subtitle: "Use low-rank GLM sketches and residual autocorrelation diagnostics."
jupyter: python3
execute:
  freeze: auto
---

## Why Sketch?

For large fMRI matrices, randomized sketches can reduce the temporal or
spatial problem size before solving the GLM.

```{python}
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import fmrimod as fm
from fmrimod.lowrank import LowRankConfig, fit_sketched

rng = np.random.default_rng(14)
n_time = 180
n_voxels = 30

time = np.linspace(-1, 1, n_time)
X = np.column_stack([
    np.ones(n_time),
    time,
    np.sin(np.linspace(0, 4 * np.pi, n_time)),
])
beta = rng.normal(size=(X.shape[1], n_voxels))
Y = X @ beta + rng.normal(scale=0.2, size=(n_time, n_voxels))

full = fm.fmri_ols_fit(Y, X)
sketched = fit_sketched(X, Y, LowRankConfig(sketch_ratio=0.55, seed=1))

full["beta"].shape, sketched.betas.shape
```

## Compare Estimates

```{python}
comparison = pd.DataFrame({
    "full_beta": full["beta"].ravel(),
    "sketched_beta": sketched.betas.ravel(),
})

comparison.corr().iloc[0, 1].round(4)
```

## Residual Autocorrelation

Temporal correlation should be inspected on residuals, especially before
leaning on standard errors.

```{python}
residuals = Y - X @ full["beta"]
acf = fm.acorr_diagnostics(residuals, max_lag=8)

pd.DataFrame({"lag": acf["lags"], "acf": acf["acf"]}).round(3)
```

## AR Noise Fixture

```{python}
ar_resid = fm.simulate_noise_vector(
    n_time,
    tr=2.0,
    ar=[0.5],
    ma=[],
    drift_amplitude=0.0,
    physio=False,
    seed=9,
)

fm.acorr_diagnostics(ar_resid, max_lag=3)["acf"].round(3)
```

## Next

- [Linear model](linear-model.qmd)
- [Simulation](simulation.qmd)
